text: Setting clear goals is essential for any successful project. A useful method is to set SMART goals, which means they should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase page load speed by 20% within three months." Next, break your project into milestones, like completing a prototype or finishing user testing. Each milestone should lead to a deliverable, such as a report or a working feature. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing these goals with everyone involved. When team members and clients agree on the objectives, the project runs more smoothly and avoids misunderstandings.
